News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Passage through the Strait of Hormuz by a Japan-related vessel Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 29 Apr 2026

  • Iran-U.S. diplomatic impasse continues despite Tehran's new proposal to end war People's Daily 29 Apr 2026 -- Iran's new proposal to the United States to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the two-month war -- while postponing nuclear negotiations to a later stage -- has failed to produce any breakthrough in the bilateral diplomatic impasse amid mounting global calls for de-escalation.

  • Trump Tells Putin To Focus On Ending Ukraine War After Russian President Offers Help On Iran's Uranium RFE/RL 29 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said Russian leader Vladimir Putin made proposals regarding Iran's enriched uranium -- a key issue to any US-Iran peace deal -- but Trump said he would prefer that the Kremlin focus first on ending its war with Ukraine.
  • Trump Says Iran 'Better Get Smart Soon' RFE/RL 29 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump asserted that Iran is incapable of reaching a non-nuclear deal.
  • Trump Says US Maintains Negotiations With Iran Via Phone Calls Sputnik 29 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that the United States maintains a dialogue with Iran by phone calls.
  • Russia-Iran Axis Expanding The Battlefield, US Senator Thom Tillis Warns RFE/RL 29 Apr 2026 -- Senator Thom Tillis, a co-chair of the Senate NATO Observer Group and a self-described "plain-spoken" Republican from North Carolina, has a message for those who view the conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East as isolated events: they are not.
  • Hegseth Defends War With Iran, Lashes Out At Democrats RFE/RL 29 Apr 2026 -- US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth defended the war with Iran in a fiery speech to Congress on April 29, insisting it had "not turned into a quagmire."
  • Pentagon chief grilled in Congress over huge cost of war against Iran Press TV 29 Apr 2026 -- US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth was grilled by members of Congress over the enormous cost of the joint American-Israeli military aggression against Iran.
  • Pentagon: US War With Iran Has Cost $25 Billion RFE/RL 29 Apr 2026 -- A senior US Defense Department official announced that the cost of the country's war with Iran has reached $25 billion so far, a figure that is considered the first official estimate of the costs of the conflict.
  • National Guard Military Police Battalion Deploys in Support of Operation Epic Fury DoD News 29 Apr 2026 -- Approximately 150 soldiers assigned to Headquarters and Headquarters Company, 192nd Military Police Battalion, Connecticut Army National Guard, participated in a deployment ceremony at Bradley Air National Guard Base, East Granby, Connecticut, yesterday.

  • Iran's Conservative Camp Split Over US Talks To End War RFE/RL 29 Apr 2026 -- In the face of a devastating US-Israeli bombing campaign, Iran's rival political factions cast aside their differences to unite behind the country's decision-makers.
  • 'Endgame' For Iran's Oil Sector? How US Blockade May Impact Tehran's Calculus RFE/RL 29 Apr 2026 -- Weeks of US and Israeli air strikes, sanctions, and restrictions have hit Iran hard, but it could be geology that eventually pushes it into making concessions in its ongoing standoff with the United States.
  • New round of war to be catastrophic for US: Leader's advisor Press TV 29 Apr 2026 -- A senior advisor to Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei says fresh aggression towards Iran will be "catastrophic" for the United States.
  • April 29: Iran defies US blockade as national unity surges while crisis deepens in Washington Press TV 29 Apr 2026 -- A senior Iranian security source, speaking to Press TV, warned that the US "naval piracy and banditry" will soon face "practical and unprecedented response," as millions filled the streets nationwide to reaffirm allegiance to the Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Sayyed Mojtaba Khamenei, on the birth anniversary of Imam Reza (AS).
  • Iran to soon unveil a weapon the enemy is 'deeply afraid of': Navy commander Press TV 29 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Navy Commander Rear Admiral Shahram Irani says the Islamic Republic will "very soon" confront the enemy with a weapon "it is deeply afraid of."
  • Qalibaf: Iranian nation will defeat US plots to sow internal division Press TV 29 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf says the Iranian nation will once again defeat US plots aimed at sowing division in the country through unity and faith.
  • Iran has every right to counter US's state-sponsored piracy, terrorism: UN envoy Press TV 29 Apr 2026 -- Iran's ambassador to the United Nations underlines that the Islamic Republic reserves all rights to duly confront the United States' seizure of Iranian vessels as part of Washington's continued illegal blockade against the country.

  • UN rights chief warns of escalating crackdown in Iran amid conflict UN News 29 Apr 2026 -- The UN's top human rights official warned on Wednesday that Iranians' rights are being eroded in "harsh and brutal ways," citing a surge in executions, mass arrests and alleged abuses amid a widening crackdown on dissent during the ongoing conflict.
  • 2015 nuclear deal 'no basis' for any new agreement with Iran UN News 29 Apr 2026 -- The 2015 nuclear accord with Iran cannot be the starting point for a new agreement with the country, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) said on Wednesday in New York.
  • UN: Iran Executed 21 People, Arrested Over 4,000 Since Escalation of Conflict RFE/RL 29 Apr 2026 -- Iran has executed at least 21 people and detained more than 4,000 more since the escalation of conflict in the Middle East, the United Nations Human Rights Office said.
  • Iran: Türk deplores crackdown on dissent, says rights of all Iranians must be respected OHCHR 29 Apr 2026 -- UN Human Rights Chief Volker Türk today deplored the crackdown by Iranian authorities on dissent - in particular through the use of national security-related charges. At least 21 individuals have been executed and thousands of others arrested.

  • Foreign minister's call with head of Iranian diplomacy Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 29 Apr 2026 -- 29 April 2026 Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Poland Radosław Sikorski held a phone conversation with Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Islamic Republic of Iran Abbas Araghchi. The consultations centred on today's international security challenges and the prospects for enhanced bilateral relations.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Reauthorizations Accelerate Tech for War Department DoD News 29 Apr 2026 -- The recent reauthorization of a couple of small business technology programs opens the gates for the War Department to get cutting-edge technology more quickly into the hands of warfighters.
  • Hegseth, Caine Say Budget Increases Lethality, Strengthens Defense Industrial Base DoD News 29 Apr 2026 -- The War Department's $1.5 trillion fiscal year 2027 budget request reflects the urgency of the moment, addressing both the deferment of long-standing problems, as well as positioning the joint force for both the current and the future fight, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth said today during a House Armed Services Committee hearing in Washington.
  • Socom Keeps America Safe With Wide Focus DoD News 29 Apr 2026 -- America's Special Operations Command has eyes and ears around the world focused on a variety of hotspots and threats that may put the well-being and safety of the homeland at risk.
  • Marines Debut New Ground Sensor Systems DoD News 29 Apr 2026 -- Low crawling through the woods for long periods of time is nothing new to Marine Corps Cpl. Joshua Trutna. Splinters, fatigue and heat are all part of the job as a tactical remote sensor system maintainer assigned to the Ground Sensor Platoon, 2nd Intelligence Battalion, II Marine Expeditionary Force Information Group.
  • US Marines debut new ground sensor systems during African Lion 2026 in Tunisia Army News 29 Apr 2026 -- Low crawling through the woods for long periods of time is nothing new to U.S. Marine Corps Cpl. Joshua Trutna. Splinters, fatigue and heat are all part of the job as a tactical remote sensor system maintainer with Ground Sensor Platoon, 2nd Intelligence Battalion, II Marine Expeditionary Force Information Group.
  • USAG Ansbach Demolishes and Remediates Fuel Point at Barton Barracks to Prepare for New Maintenance Facility Army News 29 Apr 2026 -- U.S. Army Garrison Ansbach recently completed the demolition of a fuel point at Barton Barracks, paving the way for the construction of a new Tactical Equipment Maintenance Facility (TEMF) to support the 1-57 Air Defense Artillery Regiment.
  • USS San Antonio Returns to Norfolk from U.S. 4th Fleet Deployment US Navy 29 Apr 2026 -- First-in-class San Antonio-class amphibious transport dock ship USS San Antonio (LPD 17) returned to its homeport in Norfolk, Virginia, April 28, 2026, after eight and a half months at sea supporting the Iwo Jima Amphibious Ready Group (IWO ARG) and the 22nd Marine Expeditionary Unit (MEU)-Special Operations Capable (SOC) mission in the U.S. 4th Fleet area of operations.
  • Senate Blocks Bid to Limit Trump's Power to Invade Cuba Sputnik 29 Apr 2026 -- The US Senate has rejected a measure that sought to restrict President Donald Trump's ability to launch military action against Cuba without congressional approval.
  • Trump's 'Golden Dome' offers 'limited' shield against ballistic missiles: Defense official Press TV 29 Apr 2026 -- A recent US Senate hearing has exposed mounting concerns that America's homeland missile defenses are dangerously misaligned with modern warfare, according to a report.
  • AFRICOM hosts leaders as part of ELDP training AFRICOM 29 Apr 2026 -- A cohort of 64 military and civilian employees taking part in a 10-month Executive Leadership Development Program (ELDP) from across the defense enterprise gained a front-line perspective of the warfighter during a visit to U.S. Africa Command in Stuttgart, Germany, April 30, 2026.

  • Visit of Commander, JFC Norfolk: Iceland's key role in North Atlantic Defence Cooperation Government of Iceland 29 Apr 2026 -- Foreign Minister Þorgerður Katrín Gunnarsdóttir met with Vice Admiral Douglas G. Perry, Commander of NATO's Joint Force Command Norfolk.

  • Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov meets with Minister of National Defence of Republic of Congo Raymond Zephirin Mboulou Russia MoD 29 Apr 2026 -- During the visit to the Russian Federation of the official delegation of the Republic of the Congo headed by President of the Republic Denis Sassou Nguesso, who arrived to meet with President of the Russian Federation Vladimir Putin, the talks between the two defence ministers were held.
  • Russia Scales Back Victory Day Parade, Citing Ukrainian Drone Attacks RFE/RL 29 Apr 2026 -- Russia's Defense Ministry said the Victory Day parade on Red Square -- an annual Kremlin spectacle celebrating the Soviet role in World War II and Moscow's military today -- will be drastically scaled back this year, with no military vehicles or heavy weaponry on display for the first time in almost two decades.
  • Statement by the Member States of the Collective Security Treaty Organization at the 2026 Review Conference of the Treaty on the Non‑Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons, New York, 28 April 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 29 Apr 2026

  • 15,000 vacancies: how to join the Drone Line and stop the enemy within the kill zone Ukraine MOD 29 Apr 2026 -- Unmanned technologies have become a key factor in modern warfare. The Drone Line project is intended to align innovation with the needs of the military, while those willing to join the Defence Forces can find their place within this system.

  • KMT deputy chair attacks Speaker Han over special defense budget CNA 29 Apr 2026 -- A deputy chair of the opposition Kuomintang (KMT) threatened at a meeting Wednesday to have Legislative Speaker Han Kuo-yu (韓國瑜) expelled from the party over his reported support for a NT$800 billion (US$25.31 billion) special defense budget, prompting party Chair Cheng Li-wun (鄭麗文) to step in to try to smooth things over.
  • U.S. House panel approves spending bill with US$500 million for Taiwan CNA 29 Apr 2026 -- A U.S. House committee on Tuesday approved a key spending bill that includes US$500 million in military assistance for Taiwan and support for its participation in international organizations.

  • Regiment restores assault pioneer capability Australia DOD 29 Apr 2026 -- Soldiers from the 1st Battalion, Royal Australian Regiment (1RAR), have reintroduced the 'assault pioneer' capability through a course to restore breaching and mobility skills within the battalion.
  • Strengthening Australian and Malaysian naval ties Australia DOD 29 Apr 2026 -- KD Lekir and HMAS Warramunga arrived at HMAS Stirling in Western Australia recently to complete the bilateral Malaysia-Australia Training Exercise (MASTEX).

  • All peace-loving countries must firmly resist Japan's reckless moves of neo-militarism: FM spokesperson People's Daily 29 Apr 2026 -- All peace-loving countries in the world must remain highly vigilant and firmly resist Japan's reckless moves of neo-militarism, said Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson Lin Jian on Tuesday.

  • DRDO & Indian Navy successfully conduct maiden salvo launch of Naval Anti-ship Missile-Short Range India PIB 29 Apr 2026 -- Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO) and Indian Navy have successfully conducted the maiden salvo launch of Naval Anti-ship Missile-Short Range (NASM-SR) from the Navy's helicopter platform off the coast of Bay of Bengal in Odisha. During the trial, two missiles were launched in quick succession from the same helicopter, making it the first salvo launch of an advanced air-launched anti-ship missile system.
  • INDIA AND SRI LANKA DEEPEN MARITIME COOPERATION THROUGH IN-SLN DIVEX 2026 India PIB 29 Apr 2026 -- India and Sri Lanka reaffirmed their growing maritime partnership through the fourth edition of the bilateral diving exercise, IN-SLN DIVEX 2026, conducted at Colombo from 21-28 Apr 2026. The exercise witnessed participation of the Indian Navy's Diving Support and Submarine Rescue Vessel, INS Nireekshak, alongside diving teams from both navies-underscoring enhanced operational coordination and professional synergy.

  • Israel's Air and Sea Bridge Expands: Two Ships and Multiple Cargo Aircraft Deliver Approximately 6,500 Tons of Munitions and Military Equipment to Israel Within 24 Hours Israel MOD 29 Apr 2026 -- In a large-scale global logistics operation, two cargo ships have docked at the Ports of Ashdod and Haifa, carrying thousands of air and ground munitions, military trucks, Joint Light Tactical Vehicles (JLTVs), and additional military equipment.

  • President urges self-reliant defense from world's No. 5 military KOREA.net 29 Apr 2026 -- President Lee Jae Myung on April 28 stressed his commitment to a strong national defense, saying, "A nation should defend itself, don't depend on others."

  • UK tests defence supply chains under war conditions in major exercise UK MOD 29 Apr 2026 -- Major wargame exercise brings together leading defence companies to test supply chain resilience under wartime pressure.

  • Professionalism of Azerbaijan Army's cybersecurity specialists enhanced Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 29 Apr 2026 -- The "Strategy of the Republic of Azerbaijan on Information Security and Cybersecurity for 2023-2027," approved by the relevant Order of President Ilham Aliyev, identifies strengthening personnel training in information security and cybersecurity, along with staffing specialized structural units with qualified personnel, as key priority areas, the Ministry told AZERTAC.

  • EDA partners with the European Commission on BraveTech EU European Defence Agency 29 Apr 2026 -- The European Defence Agency (EDA) and the European Commission signed an agreement on 29 April giving the Agency responsibility for the next phase of BraveTech EU, a joint initiative between the European Union and Ukraine.

  • Estonian and Norwegian foreign ministers reaffirm shared commitment to strengthening regional and Euro-Atlantic security Estonia MFA 29 Apr 2026 -- Today, 29 April, Estonia's Minister of Foreign Affairs Margus Tsahkna met in Tallinn with Norway's Minister of Foreign Affairs Espen Barth Eide to discuss security and resilience in the Baltic Sea region, continued support for Ukraine, increasing pressure on Russia, and issues related to NATO and transatlantic security.
  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna to Lithuanian Foreign Minister: continued support for Ukraine and EU enlargement are central to European security Estonia MFA 29 Apr 2026 -- Today, 29 April, Estonia's Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna met in Tallinn with Lithuania's Minister of Foreign Affairs Kęstutis Budrys to discuss European Union enlargement, continued support for Ukraine, regional security, and the impact of transatlantic relations on our region. The ministers emphasised that Estonia and Lithuania share a common understanding that strengthening European security requires both steadfast support for Ukraine and determined progress in the EU enlargement process.

  • A look at the Defence news 20 - 28 April Netherlands Ministry of Defence 29 Apr 2026

  • Focus on Ukraine, NATO and Middle East as Maria Malmer Stenergard attends NB8 meeting in Estonia Government Offices of Sweden 29 Apr 2026

  • PH capable of boosting missile firepower of allies - PAF Philippine News Agency 29 Apr 2026 -- The Philippine Air Force (PAF) on Wednesday said the participation of its 960th Air and Missile Defense Wing (AMDW) in the Balikatan "Integrated Air and Missile Defense" (IAMD) drills on April 26 to 29 showcased its capability to boost the defensive missile firepower of allied forces.
  • PH Army eyes stronger defense alliance with Canadian counterpart Philippine News Agency 29 Apr 2026 -- The Philippine Army (PA) is eyeing a strengthened defense alliance and cooperation with its Canadian counterpart to ensure peace and stability in the Indo-Pacific Region.
  • PH, US troops hold air defense drills for 'Balikatan' Philippine News Agency 29 Apr 2026 -- Filipino and American troops carried out integrated air and defense missile exercises in the ongoing "Balikatan" (shoulder-to-shoulder), the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) said Wednesday.

  • Poland at Review Conference of Treaty on Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 29 Apr 2026 -- Undersecretary of State Robert Kupiecki is the head of the Polish delegation at the Review Conference of the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT), held in New York. At the beginning of the Conference, Deputy Minister Kupiecki took part in high-level debate and held several bilateral meetings, including with deputy UN Secretary-General.

  • Defence Minister's meeting with the Secretary of the United States Army Romania - Ministry of National Defence 29 Apr 2026 -- On Monday, April 27th, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, received, at the institution's headquarters, the visit of an American delegation led by Daniel P. Driscoll, Secretary of the United States Army.

  • Hybrid War At NATO's Door: Lawmaker Wesley Bell On The Baltics, Russia, And US Resolve RFE/RL 29 Apr 2026 -- With US and global attention divided among multiple geopolitical theaters, a bill focused on the security of NATO's northeast flank, the Baltic states, has advanced in the House of Representatives.

  • United States Welcomes Morocco Signing of the Artemis Accords US Dept. of State 29 Apr 2026
  • The United States expressed on Wednesday "delight" to see its alliance with the Kingdom of Morocco expand to space. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 29 Apr 2026
  • New Era of Strategic Partnership: United States Advances Key Investments and Cooperation at the Three Seas Initiative Summit US Dept. of State 29 Apr 2026 -- Under Secretary of State for Political Affairs Allison Hooker and U.S. Ambassador to Croatia Nicole McGraw accompanied U.S. Head of Delegation Secretary of Energy Chris Wright to the Three Seas Initiative (3SI) Summit and Business Forum in Dubrovnik, Croatia, April 28-29, 2026.
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with UK Foreign Secretary Cooper US Dept. of State 29 Apr 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 29 April 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 29 Apr 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • MFA Statement Israel MFA 29 Apr 2026 -- Israel urges the participants not to violate the lawfully enforced blockade. The participants are further urged to alter course, and to enable the peaceful and secure transfer of any aid, if any exists, through the established international channels.

  • Israeli airstrike kills head of Hamas Military Intelligence Operations Department Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 29 Apr 2026 -- The Israel Defense Forces confirmed on Wednesday the death of senior Hamas commander Iyad Shambari in an airstrike in the northern Gaza Strip, according to TPS-IL.

  • Israeli strikes kill 10 in southern Lebanon, including 3 rescue workers Press TV 29 Apr 2026 -- At least ten people, including three rescue workers, have been killed in Israeli airstrikes against residential neighborhoods in southern Lebanon, marking the latest violations of a ceasefire that began on April 16 after weeks of fighting between the Tel Aviv regime and Hezbollah.

  • Gaza damage assessment must require end to occupation and discrimination of Palestinians: UN experts OHCHR 29 Apr 2026 -- UN experts* today expressed grave concern about the Gaza Rapid Damage and Needs Assessment, documenting housing and infrastructure destruction on an historic scale.
  • Lebanon: New IPC analysis shows conflict escalation pushing nearly a quarter of population into acute food insecurity WFP 29 Apr 2026 -- A sharp escalation in violence has reversed recent food security gains in Lebanon and pushed the country back into crisis. This is according to the latest Integrated Food Security Phase Classification (IPC) projected analysis released by the Ministry of Agriculture, in collaboration with the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) and the World Food Programme (WFP).
  • UNRWA Situation Report #218 on the Humanitarian Crisis in the Occupied Palestinian Territory (the Gaza Strip and the West Bank, including East Jerusalem) UNRWA 29 Apr 2026
  • UNRWA Situation Report #8 on the Lebanon Emergency Response 2026 (23 April 2026) UNRWA 29 Apr 2026

  • Israel pursues perpetual war and territorial encroachment agenda, with no vision for peace: Aboul Gheit before the Security Council League of Arab States 29 Apr 2026 -- Mr. Ahmed Aboul Gheit, Secretary-General of the League of Arab States, participated on 28 April in the Security Council open debate held at United Nations Headquarters in New York on the situation in the Middle East. In his address, he noted that the Arab region is currently experiencing an exceptionally tense situation due to the ongoing war and its adverse repercussions, explaining that Arab States did not call for war or military confrontation, but rather sought to avoid it. Nevertheless, they have not been spared a wave of unjustified and unlawful Iranian attacks.
  • China calls for efforts to restore stability in Mideast Global Times 29 Apr 2026 -- A Chinese envoy on Tuesday called on parties in the Middle East conflict to strive for the early restoration of stability in the region.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Somalia, Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Democratic Republic of the Congo UNOCHA 29 Apr 2026 -- UN Relief Chief warns funding cuts pushing most vulnerable to the brink

  • Press release of the Secretariat of the State Commission on the Delimitation of the State Border between the Republic of Azerbaijan and the Republic of Armenia Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 29 Apr 2026 -- On 29 April 2026, in Aghveran, Republic of Armenia, the thirteenth meeting of the State Commission on the Delimitation of the State Border between the Republic of Azerbaijan and the Republic of Armenia and the Commission on Delimitation of the State Border and Border Security Issues between the Republic of Armenia and the Republic of Azerbaijan was held under the chairmanship of the Deputy Prime Minister of the Republic of Azerbaijan, Shahin Mustafayev, and the Deputy Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia, Mher Grigoryan.

  • Duterte case: ICC Presidency constitutes Trial Chamber III ICC 29 Apr 2026 -- On 24 April 2026, the Presidency of the International Criminal Court (ICC) constituted Trial Chamber III, which will be in charge of the case of The Prosecutor v. Rodrigo Roa Duterte. Trial Chamber III is composed of Presiding Judge Joanna Korner, Judge Keebong Paek and Judge Nicolas Guillou.

  • The United States reaffirmed, on Wednesday, its "recognition of Morocco's sovereignty over the Sahara." Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 29 Apr 2026

  • Morocco has reiterated its support for Mali's sovereignty and national unity, said on Wednesday in Rabat the Minister of Foreign Affairs, African Cooperation and Moroccan Expatriates, Mr. Nasser Bourita. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 29 Apr 2026

  • AFP chief lauds military units for commitment to peace in Mindanao Philippine News Agency 29 Apr 2026 -- Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) chief Gen. Romeo Brawner Jr. has lauded the 1st Infantry Division for its role in ensuring that peace and development continue in Mindanao.

  • EU Statement on the Secretary General's thirty-third consolidated report on the conflict in Georgia European External Action Service (EEAS) 29 Apr 2026

News Reports

  • Chairman Crawford Statement on House Passage of FISA 702 Reform & Reauthorization Bill US House of Representatives Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence 29 Apr 2026 -- House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence Chairman Rick Crawford (AR-01) released the following statement after the House of Representatives passed S. 1318, the Foreign Intelligence Accountability Act, to reauthorize and reform FISA Section 702.

  • Federal Jury Convicts ISIS-K Terrorist for Role in the Abbey Gate Bombing and Other ISIS-K Attacks US Dept. of Justice 29 Apr 2026 -- A federal jury today convicted Afghan national Mohammad Sharifullah, a member of the terrorist organization the Islamic State of Iraq and ash-Sham-Khorasan Province (ISIS-K), of participating in a nine-year conspiracy to provide material support and resources to a designated foreign terrorist organization.

  • Commentary by Aide to the President of Russia Yury Ushakov following Vladimir Putin's telephone conversation with President of the United States Donald Trump President of Russia 29 Apr 2026
  • Meeting with Head of the Chechen Republic Ramzan Kadyrov President of Russia 29 Apr 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held a working meeting with Head of the Chechen Republic Ramzan Kadyrov at the Kremlin.

  • Wang Yi Holds China-Australia Foreign and Strategic Dialogue with Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong PRC MofA 29 Apr 2026 -- On April 29, 2026, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi held the eighth China-Australia Foreign and Strategic Dialogue with Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong in Beijing.
  • Wang Yi Meets with President of the 80th Session of the United Nations General Assembly Annalena Baerbock PRC MofA 29 Apr 2026 -- On April 29, 2026, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi met with President of the 80th Session of the United Nations General Assembly Annalena Baerbock in Beijing.
  • China's Energy Giant Announces Earnings Boost As Iran War Ripples Through Chinese Economy RFE/RL 29 Apr 2026 -- Sinopec, China's state-owned oil and natural gas giant, announced stronger-than-projected first-quarter earnings buoyed by a surge in energy prices from the war in Iran.

  • Cross-Straits affairs are internal family matters that should be handled through consultation, Taiwan Affairs Office responds to KMT head's reported US visit Global Times 29 Apr 2026 -- Chinese compatriots on both sides of the Taiwan Straits are all Chinese and one family. Matters concerning the two sides of the Straits are the internal affairs of the Chinese family, and of course should be discussed and handled through consultation among members of the family themselves, said Chen Binhua, spokesperson of State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, at a press conference on Wednesday.
  • 'Absurd' to obstruct mainland tech in Taiwan because it's popular: State Council Taiwan Affairs Office on DPP threatening to ban Amap Global Times 29 Apr 2026 -- Simply because mainland technologies and products are effective and popular among the people of Taiwan, the Taiwan authorities seek to ban or obstruct their use. This is an example of jittery with imaginary fear - absurd and ridiculous. Such move are unpopular and will not succeed, Chen Binhua, spokesperson for the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, said on Wednesday in response to a question about the mainland app Amap becoming popular on the Taiwan island while the DPP has raised so-called security concerns over it.
  • DPP has only itself to blame, brought embarrassment upon itself: State Council Taiwan Affairs Office spokesperson on Lai's aborted visit Global Times 29 Apr 2026 -- The DPP has only itself to blame and has brought this embarrassment upon itself, Chen Binhua, spokesperson for the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, said on Wednesday in response to a question about Taiwan regional leader Lai Ching-te being denied entry during a planned overseas trip.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 29 Apr 2026

  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Enjoys Concert Marking 80th Founding Anniversary of Central Military Band of Ministry of National Defence KCNA 29 Apr 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un , General Secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and President of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, enjoyed a concert commemorating the 80th founding anniversary of the Central Military Band of the Ministry of National Defence on April 28.

  • Policeman killed, three wounded in terrorist shooting in southeast Iran Press TV 29 Apr 2026 -- Armed terrorists opened fire on a police patrol that was providing security for citizens in the city of Zahedan, Tasnim news agency reported on Wednesday.

  • Netanyahu uses aid flotilla as excuse to cut short corruption court session Press TV 29 Apr 2026 -- Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu has shortened a court hearing held to examine his corruption charges to attend an alleged security meeting dedicated to an aid flotilla heading toward the Gaza Strip.

  • Madagascar - Summoning of the Charge d'Affaires of the Madagascar embassy France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 29 Apr 2026 -- On Tuesday 28 April the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs summoned the Chargé d'Affaires of the Madagascar Embassy in Paris to protest strenuously against the declaration of a diplomatic employee at the French Embassy in Antananarivo as "persona non grata".

  • Economy and security: Foreign Minister Wadephul travels to Morocco for strategic dialogue Germany Federal Foreign Office 29 Apr 2026 -- Be it the Middle East conflict or the Sahel crisis - Morocco plays a key role due to its channels of communication. Moreover, the country is our second largest trading partner on the African continent. We want to further exploit this potential. Read on to find out more about the strategic dialogue.
  • Statement by Foreign Minister Wadephul prior to his departure for Morocco Germany Federal Foreign Office 29 Apr 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with German Foreign Minister Wadephul US Dept. of State 29 Apr 2026

  • Prime Minister Carney speaks with Crown Prince and Prime Minister of Saudi Arabia His Royal Highness Mohammed bin Salman Prime Minister of Canada 29 Apr 2026 -- Today, the Prime Minister, Mark Carney, spoke with the Crown Prince and Prime Minister of Saudi Arabia, His Royal Highness Mohammed bin Salman.

  • Explainer: What to know about UAE exit from OPEC, OPEC+ People's Daily 29 Apr 2026 -- The United Arab Emirates (UAE) on Tuesday announced its decision to quit the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) and the wider OPEC+ alliance, effective on May 1, following "a careful look at the regional power's energy strategies."

  • Iran crisis shows fossil fuels-based energy system 'is inherently volatile and unstable' UN News 29 Apr 2026 -- Senior UN climate leaders are getting behind countries mobilising this week for a transition away from fossil fuels at a groundbreaking event in Colombia.

  • UN80 Initiative: Real progress made on tackling complex issues facing the UN system UN News 29 Apr 2026 -- A new guide to the UN80 Initiative, released on Wednesday, provides an overview of the progress being made so far on this ambitious, wide-ranging reform effort.

  • Russia says UAE withdrawal from OPEC, OPEC+ is 'sovereign decision' Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 29 Apr 2026 -- Russia said Wednesday that the United Arab Emirates' withdrawal from the OPEC oil producer group and OPEC+ is a "sovereign decision," according to Anadolu Agency.

  • Armenia/Türkiye: Statement by the Spokesperson on the talks about the restoration of rail connection between the two countries European External Action Service (EEAS) 29 Apr 2026 -- The EU welcomes yesterday's meeting of the Armenia-Türkiye bilateral working group in Kars to advance the restoration of rail connections between the two countries.

  • House panel finds probable cause to impeach VP Duterte Philippine News Agency 29 Apr 2026 -- The House Committee on Justice on Wednesday found probable cause to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte.
  • NBI: VP Sara may be convicted for sedition, grave threats Philippine News Agency 29 Apr 2026 -- The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has found a "reasonable certainty of conviction" against Vice President Sara Duterte for inciting to sedition and multiple counts of grave threats, based on statements she made in a Nov. 23, 2024 press conference.

  • Norway provides NOK 1.7 billion to the World Food Programme to combat hunger and humanitarian need Government of Norway 29 Apr 2026 -- Minister of International Development Åsmund Aukrust today signed a new four-year agreement with the World Food Programme (WFP), committing NOK 1.72 billion to help people affected by hunger. The funding will contribute to saving lives at a time when the number of people facing acute food insecurity is at a record high.

  • World News in Brief: Europe warming twice as fast, acute hunger in Lebanon, peacekeepers deployed in Congo UN News 29 Apr 2026 -- New data shows record heatwaves from the Mediterranean to the Arctic last year, with glaciers shrinking and snow cover declining, according to the UN World Meteorological Organization (WMO).
